title: Dermatologic Emergencies

vignette.text: A 55-year-old diabetic presents with rapidly spreading erythema on his leg with severe pain, fever, and crepitus on exam.

questions.question: This presentation suggests?
questions.explanation: Necrotizing fasciitis: pain out of proportion, rapid spread, crepitus, systemic toxicity. Surgical emergency.

keyTakeaways: Nec fasc: pain out of proportion, crepitus, rapid spread
Pemphigus vulgaris: oral erosions, flaccid bullae, + Nikolsky
Most require ICU and specialist care
